历年位次与招生计划.title: Historical Rankings and Enrollment Plan
历年位次与招生计划.text: From 2021 to 2025, the minimum rankings for Physics Category were 5596 → 6764 → 8933 → 10067 → 9900, showing a continuous backward trend overall, with a cumulative backward shift of about 4300 positions in the past 4 years; the 2025 baseline is approximately 9900, a slight recovery compared to 2024. The university's enrollment plan for Physics Category in Fujian Province was 10 in 2021, 11 from 2022 to 2024, 12 in 2025, and is planned to be sharply reduced to 8 in 2026, a decrease of 4 from 2025 (a 33.3% reduction), significantly intensifying competition.

学校层次与声誉.title: University Level and Reputation
学校层次与声誉.text: Beijing Foreign Studies University is a 211 Project and Double First-Class construction university; it ranked 79th in the 2026 Soft Science Chinese University Rankings, with stable rankings in the 73–82 range in recent years, making it a top-tier language institution; its reputation evaluation in 2023 was somewhat negative, with a noticeable decline in the admission positions for minor language majors; the 2026 reputation evaluation remains somewhat negative; its overall Douyin popularity ranking is 39th (relatively hot), with moderate overall attention but weak reputation signals in recent years; Foreign Language and Literature is an A+ discipline, top in the country, directly corresponding to language majors such as Translation, Japanese, and Russian offered in the Physics Category.

专业热度与地域.title: Major Popularity and Region
专业热度与地域.text: In 2024, the popularity of foreign language majors was assessed as "generally wait-and-see," while majors like Computer Science and Finance are more popular but not core strengths of BFSU; the total number of regular college entrance exam takers in Fujian Province in 2026 is approximately 267,900, with Physics Category students estimated at about 204,000 (an increase of about 6.2% from approximately 192,000 in 2025), and the expansion of the candidate pool intensifies competition. Beijing Foreign Studies University is located in Beijing, making it an inter-provincial application for Fujian candidates; the significant reduction in the Physics Category enrollment plan in 2026 has a notable impact on rankings.

预测结论.title: Prediction Conclusion
预测结论.text: Considering the 2025 baseline ranking of 9900, the 2026 Physics Category enrollment plan reduced from 12 to 8 (a 33.3% reduction), the increase in Fujian Physics Category candidates by about 6.2% intensifying competition, and the generally moderate popularity of foreign language majors but the continued appeal of BFSU's A+ disciplines, it is predicted that the minimum admission ranking for Physics Category in 2026 will significantly move forward compared to 2025 (competition intensifies, ranking number decreases). The predicted minimum ranking is approximately 8050, with a range of 7800–8300.

If your ranking is significantly better than the lower limit of the range (e.g., within 7800), you can aim for popular majors like Translation and Computer Science; if your ranking is near the upper limit (around 8300), it is advisable to use the Russian dual-degree program as a safety. BFSU's Physics Category enrollment plan in 2026 is sharply reduced, intensifying competition, so applicants need to position themselves reasonably.

历年位次与招生计划.title: Historical Rankings and Enrollment Plan
历年位次与招生计划.text: From 2021 to 2025, the minimum rankings for History Category were 1164 → 1292 → 1719 → 1561 → 1435, showing a fluctuating and backward trend overall. In 2023, due to the cooling of minor language majors, the ranking dropped significantly to 1719, but rebounded in 2024–2025, with a baseline of about 1435 in 2025. The enrollment plan for this university in Fujian Province for History Category was 14 in 2021, 11 in 2022, and 13 for three consecutive years from 2023 to 2025. The 2026 plan remains 13, unchanged from 2025, with no expansion or reduction at the plan level.

学校层次与声誉.title: University Level and Reputation
学校层次与声誉.text: Beijing Foreign Studies University is a 211 Project and Double First-Class university. In the 2026 Soft Science China University Rankings, it ranks 79th, with a stable range of 73–82 in recent years, making it a top-tier language institution. In 2023, its reputation evaluation was somewhat negative, with a noticeable decline in the admission positions of minor language majors. In 2026, the reputation evaluation remains somewhat negative, involving academic misconduct and teacher ethics incidents. Its Douyin popularity ranks 39th overall (relatively hot), and it once rose to 12th in 2024 (very hot), indicating moderate attention but weak reputation signals in recent years. Foreign Language and Literature is an A+ discipline, nationally top-tier, directly corresponding to all language-related majors offered by the university, still exerting strong appeal for applicants.

专业热度与地域.title: Major Popularity and Region
专业热度与地域.text: In 2024, the popularity of foreign language majors was assessed as "generally wait-and-see," with overall low popularity. The total number of regular college entrance exam takers in Fujian Province in 2026 is about 267,900, an increase of about 6.2% from about 252,300 in 2025. History Category candidates are estimated at about 64,000 (an increase of about 6.2% from about 60,000 in 2025), with the expanded candidate pool intensifying competition. Beijing Foreign Studies University is located in Beijing, making it an out-of-province application for Fujian candidates, with no local enrollment preference. The university's enrollment plan in Fujian is stable, with no significant regional preference changes.

预测结论.title: Prediction Conclusion
预测结论.text: Considering the 2025 baseline ranking of 1435, the unchanged 2026 enrollment plan, the approximately 6.2% increase in History Category candidates in Fujian Province leading to intensified competition, the generally moderate social popularity of foreign language majors but the prominent advantage of BFSU's A+ discipline, and the somewhat weak reputation signals, it is expected that the minimum admission ranking for History Category in 2026 will slightly move forward compared to 2025 (competition slightly intensifies, ranking number decreases). The predicted minimum ranking is about 1415, with a range of 1380–1450.
